All ACs grep/count-verified; lint green. Awaiting Verified+1.

compiled_state.description:
# README front door: first-30-seconds pass for evaluators

## Why

The README serves a different persona than contributor docs: a prospective USER deciding in 30 seconds whether to try rebar (split from the onboarding story on review advice — independent actor, independently shippable). All six surveyed loved dev-tool READMEs (ruff, uv, httpx, rich, just, fzf) converge on: H1/logo → one badge row → one-sentence tagline within ~15 lines → an image within the first screen (~line 40) → highlight bullets → install → copy-pasteable quickstart, with ≤200 words of prose before the first code block. rebar's current README is information-rich but insider-shaped. Caveat acknowledged: the six exemplars are single-surface tools while rebar is multi-surface (CLI + library + MCP) — the adaptation is httpx's move (show the API fast) applied per surface, not a claim the pattern transfers verbatim.

## What

- Top-of-README restructure (content below the fold is preserved, only re-ordered/trimmed):
  1. `# rebar` → one badge row, exactly 4 badges: PyPI version, Python versions, CI status, license.
  2. One-sentence tagline naming the differentiator: an event-sourced, git-backed ticket store + Jira reconciler built for agent swarms, exposed as a Python library, CLI, and MCP server.
  3. Demo asset by ~line 40: a static terminal screenshot (decision: screenshot over gif/asciinema — zero-dependency to render on GitHub, versionable, no motion-accessibility concerns; recorded here and in the commit) of the core loop: `rebar ready` → `rebar claim` → `rebar transition … closed`. Asset committed under `docs/assets/` and referenced with a relative path.
  4. 5–8 highlight bullets; the tri-surface is one explicit bullet; the two-gate Gerrit dogfooding is another (it is the project's most distinctive practice).
  5. Install: `pipx install nava-rebar` (+ brew one-liner).
  6. Quickstart ≤15 lines total covering all three surfaces: a 3-command CLI session, a 3-line Python snippet, a one-line MCP registration.
- Keep prose before the first code block ≤200 words (uv's ratio).
- Contributor pointer near the bottom links CONTRIBUTING.md and `docs/your-first-change.md` (this story depends_on the onboarding story so the link is never dangling).

## Scope

IN: README.md top section, `docs/assets/` screenshot, badge row.
OUT: docs/ restructuring; CONTRIBUTING/tutorial content (onboarding story); marketing site; benchmark charts (rebar's pitch is workflow, not speed — a screenshot, not a bench, per the just/fzf pattern).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Badge row present with exactly 4 badges — prove: `grep -c "img.shields.io\|badge" README.md` in the first 10 lines = 4 (or equivalent count of badge images).
- [ ] Tagline within the first 15 lines states event-sourced/git-backed + agent-oriented + the three surfaces — prove: head -15 contains "MCP" and "CLI".
- [ ] Demo asset committed and referenced — prove: `test -f docs/assets/<asset>` and README references it within the first 40 lines.
- [ ] Quickstart covers CLI + Python + MCP in ≤15 total code lines — prove: count the fenced block lines.
- [ ] ≤200 words of prose before the first fenced code block — prove: word count of the pre-fence region.
- [ ] Asset-form decision (static screenshot; rationale) recorded in the commit message and a ticket comment.
compiled_state.title: README front door: first-30-seconds pass for evaluators
